sections_cited: section 133 of the Income Tax Ordinance

chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI ITRA 402 of 2024 ____________________________________________________________ DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE ____________________________________________________________ 1. For orders on office objection 2. For hearing of CMA No.3275/2024 3. For hearing of main case 10.12.2025 Mr. Taimoor Ahmed Qureshi, advocate for the applicant Mr. Asad Aftab Solangi, advocate for respondent On 03.12.2025, following order was passed: “03.12.2025 Mr. Muhammad Taimoor Ahmed, advocate for the applicant Mr. Asad Aftab Solangi, advocate for the respondent This is a case of unmerited judgment of remand by the Commissioner (Appeals). Learned counsel for the respondent seeks time to obtain instructions. At his request, to come up on 10.12.2025. Interim order passed earlier to continue till the next date of hearing” Learned counsel for respondent states that it may be just and proper and in the interest of all parties concerned, to set aside the impugned judgment and remand the matter to the learned Commissioner Appeals to decide the matter expeditiously on merits. Learned counsel for the applicant states that the reference application may be disposed off in the said terms. Order accordingly. A copy of this decision may be sent under the seal of this Court and the signature of the Registrar to the learned Appellate Tribunal, as required per section 133 of the Income Tax Ordinance, 2001. Judge Judge Amjad
